senses.definition: writing materials such as paper, pencils, notebooks, etc.

examples.x: She stocked up on stationery before the start of the school year, buying notebooks, pens, and folders.
The office manager ordered new stationery supplies, including letterhead and envelopes with the company logo.
He enjoyed browsing through the stationery aisle, admiring the variety of colorful notepads and journals.
The stationery store offered personalized stationery sets, perfect for sending handwritten notes and invitations.
She kept a drawer filled with stationery in her desk, always ready for writing letters or jotting down ideas.

senses.definition: a cover with metal rings inside that pass through the holes of the documents within to hold them together

examples.x: She organized her study notes in a ring binder with dividers for each subject.
The office supplies store offers a variety of ring binders in different colors and sizes.
He flipped through the pages of his ring binder to find the meeting agenda for the day.
Ring binders are popular for organizing documents and reports in a neat and accessible manner.
The project proposal was presented in a professional ring binder with a clear cover to showcase the title page.

senses.definition: a cover made of plastic or folded card in which documents or pieces of paper can be kept

examples.x: She organized her research papers into different folders labeled by topic for easy reference.
The presentation slides were stored in a folder with clear plastic sleeves to protect them from damage.
He kept his important documents in a sturdy folder with a secure clasp to prevent them from falling out.
The teacher handed out folders to each student to store their assignments and class notes.
Students were instructed to submit their assignments in a folder with their name and class number on it.

senses.definition: a small, thin piece of bent wire or plastic used for holding together sheets of paper

examples.x: She used a paper clip to keep the documents organized.
The report was held together with a red paper clip.
She clipped the receipt to the form with a paper clip.

senses.definition: a metal object with two toothed jaws and a spring, used for holding things in place

examples.x: He used an alligator clip to connect the wires securely during the electrical experiment.
The technician attached the alligator clip to the ground wire for testing purposes.
Alligator clips are commonly used in electronics and laboratories for temporary connections.
She labeled each alligator clip with different colors to easily identify their functions.
The alligator clip's strong grip ensured a stable connection between the components.

senses.definition: a small piece of colored paper that is sticky on one side and can be easily removed, used for leaving notes
a small board that has a clip on top for holding sheets of paper

examples.x: The nurse carried a clipboard with patient charts as she made her rounds in the hospital.
He used a clipboard to keep track of scores during the basketball game.
She held the clipboard steady while the photographer took the group photo.
The clipboard hung on a hook near the entrance, ready for visitors to sign in.
Students carried clipboards during the outdoor science experiment to record their observations.

senses.definition: a pen that can be refilled with ink

examples.x: She cherished her grandfather's fountain pen, which he used to write letters during the war.
The CEO signed the contract with a prestigious fountain pen, emphasizing the importance of the agreement.
Fountain pens are favored by writers for their smooth ink flow and ergonomic design.
He filled his fountain pen with blue ink before beginning to write in his leather-bound journal.
The calligrapher demonstrated intricate lettering techniques using a vintage fountain pen.

senses.definition: the tip of a pen that puts the ink on paper

examples.x: The calligrapher selected a fine nib to create delicate lines in the artwork.
She prefers fountain pens with broad nibs for writing bold and expressive letters.
The architect used a technical pen with a precise nib to draw detailed floor plans.
He adjusted the nib of his ballpoint pen to ensure a consistent flow of ink.
The artist experimented with different nib sizes to achieve varying textures and shading in the sketch.

senses.definition: a pen with a small metal ball at its tip that puts the ink on paper when it rolls

examples.x: The office supplies included a box of blue and black ballpoints for the staff.
He prefers using a ballpoint for filling out forms due to its smooth writing experience.
The student borrowed a red ballpoint from his classmate to complete his homework assignment.
Ballpoints are known for their reliability and durability, making them popular choices for everyday writing tasks.

senses.definition: a pen with a metal ball at the tip that when rolls puts the ink on paper

examples.x: He grabbed a biro from the drawer to write a quick note before heading out.
The journalist always carries a biro in his pocket for taking notes during interviews.
She prefers using biros with fine tips for precise writing in her planner.
The biro's ergonomic design makes it comfortable to hold during long writing sessions.

senses.definition: the object inside a pencil that makes a mark when moved on paper

examples.x: The lead in mechanical pencils is available in different diameters to suit various writing preferences.
When the lead in my pencil broke, I had to sharpen it to continue writing.
High-quality pencils often use graphite as the lead material for smooth and consistent writing.
The artist carefully selected the softness of the lead for sketching, opting for a medium grade to achieve the desired shading effects.
After running out of lead in her pencil, she reached for a spare pack to refill it and continue her work.

senses.definition: plastic tape that is transparent and sticky on one side, used for sticking things together, such as paper

examples.x: She used Scotch tape to wrap the birthday present securely.
The teacher used Scotch tape to affix posters to the classroom walls.
He sealed the envelope with a strip of Scotch tape before sending it in the mail.
Scotch tape is handy for repairing torn pages in books and notebooks.
They used Scotch tape to mark the outline of their art project on the wall.

senses.definition: a type of pen with a colored tip, used for marking words in a piece of writing, book, magazine, etc.

examples.x: She used a yellow highlighter to mark important passages in her textbook.
The editor used a pink highlighter to indicate revisions in the document.
Highlighters come in various colors to help categorize information while studying.
He borrowed a green highlighter from his colleague to emphasize key points in his presentation.
The student's notes were filled with highlights in blue and orange from different highlighter pens.

senses.definition: a pen with a tip that is made of a fiber called felt

examples.x: The artist preferred using felt tips for sketching because of their precise lines.
He grabbed a red felt tip to mark important dates on his calendar.

senses.definition: a tool used for fastening sheets of paper together by putting staples into them

examples.x: She used a stapler to bind the pages of her report together.
The office supply cabinet contained several staplers of different sizes and colors.
He refilled the stapler with staples before starting on the new batch of documents.
The stapler jammed midway through stapling, requiring him to clear the blockage.
She borrowed a heavy-duty stapler from the administrative assistant to staple the thick booklet.

senses.definition: a handheld tool with a small blade inside, used for sharpening pencils

examples.x: She used a pencil sharpener to sharpen her dull pencil before the exam.
The artist preferred using a handheld pencil sharpener for sketching on location.
The office manager replaced the old pencil sharpener with a new, more efficient model.

senses.definition: a white liquid for covering mistakes made while writing
a type of thumbtack with a colored piece of plastic on one end

examples.x: She used a pushpin to hang up the calendar on the bulletin board.
The map was dotted with pushpins marking locations they planned to visit.
He secured the poster to the wall with several colorful pushpins.
The office manager used pushpins to display important notices in the break room.
Students used pushpins to attach their artwork to the display board for the school fair.

senses.definition: a short pin with a flat round head that is used to fasten paper to a wall or board

examples.x: She pinned the memo to the bulletin board using a yellow thumbtack.
The teacher used thumbtacks to hang student artwork along the classroom walls.
He carefully removed the old calendar from the wall, pulling out each thumbtack one by one.
Thumbtacks are useful for securing fabric to a corkboard for crafting projects.
The office supply drawer was stocked with thumbtacks in various colors for organizational purposes.

senses.definition: a machine or tool used for making holes in paper or other material

examples.x: She used a three-hole punch to organize her documents into a binder.
The office manager bought a new punch for the department's paperwork needs.
He used a single-hole punch to make holes in the corners of his flashcards.
The craft store offered decorative punches for creating intricate designs in paper.
The punch on his desk had a lever for easy operation when punching through thick stacks of paper.

senses.definition: a set of sheets of paper held together on which one can write notes

examples.x: She always carries a notepad in her bag to jot down ideas throughout the day.
He kept a notepad on his bedside table to write down his dreams in the morning.
The meeting attendees each received a branded notepad and pen for taking notes.
The journalist flipped through her notepad to review the interview questions.
Students used their notepads to record lecture notes during the class.

senses.definition: a handheld tool that is used for imprinting a name, date, etc. onto a piece of paper

examples.x: The secretary stamped the document with the company's rubber stamp before filing it.
He purchased a custom rubber stamp with his initials for signing documents.
The librarian used a rubber stamp to mark the due date on borrowed books.
Rubber stamps are useful for endorsing checks and official documents.
She collected vintage rubber stamps with intricate designs for her scrapbooking hobby.

senses.definition: a pencil with a button on top that can be pushed or turned to get more lead out

examples.x: She prefers using a mechanical pencil for writing because it never needs sharpening.
The architect used a mechanical pencil to sketch the initial floor plans of the building.
He keeps a mechanical pencil in his pocket for jotting down quick notes during meetings.
Mechanical pencils come with different lead sizes to accommodate various writing preferences.
The student used a mechanical pencil for the math exam to ensure precise calculations.

senses.definition: thin paper with one side coated with a dark substance, put between two sheets of paper to copy everything that is being written or typed on it onto the sheet underneath
an alphabetically-arranged set of cards with information on them, found especially in libraries
a tray-like object on an office desk, where incoming tasks or documents are placed for one to deal with
a tray-like object on an office desk, where completed tasks, documents, etc. are placed before filing or further processing

examples.x: She placed the completed reports in the out-basket for her supervisor to review.
The assistant emptied the out-basket and filed the documents in the appropriate folders.
He checked his out-basket before leaving for the day to ensure all tasks were completed.
The out-basket on her desk contained outgoing mail ready for postage.
Employees were instructed to place finished assignments in the out-basket for collection.

senses.definition: a piece of office furniture with drawers in which documents can be stored

examples.x: She organized the client files in the bottom drawer of the file cabinet.
The file cabinet in the corner of the office contained important documents dating back several years.
He stored confidential paperwork securely in the locked file cabinet.
The administrative assistant retrieved the contract from the file cabinet for the meeting.
The office manager labeled each drawer of the file cabinet for easy access to different categories of files.

senses.definition: an object with an open top that is used for holding trash, particularly waste paper

examples.x: She tossed the crumpled paper into the wastebasket under her desk.
The office cleaner emptied the wastebaskets every evening after business hours.
He retrieved the accidentally discarded document from the wastebasket.
The wastebasket in the conference room was overflowing with used coffee cups.
Students were reminded to dispose of their trash in the classroom wastebasket.

senses.definition: functional and easy to use

examples.x: His handy toolkit contained everything he needed for small household repairs.
The handy app on her phone helped her track her daily expenses.
She found the new recipe book quite handy for quick and easy dinner ideas.
The handy gadget clipped onto his belt was a multi-tool with various functions.
Having a handy reference guide saved him time when troubleshooting computer issues.

senses.definition: made for practical use, not for looks

examples.x: The jacket is functional, with plenty of pockets for storage.
They chose a functional table that fits perfectly in small spaces.
The watch is simple and functional, ideal for everyday wear.
The functional layout of the kitchen makes cooking easier.
The design of the chair is purely functional, with no extra details.
